result:
If I could change the world in one single way, I would make every human being genuinely curious about the inner experience of others.

Not empathy as a slogan. Not "be kind" as a bumper sticker. Real, stubborn, almost painful curiosity: the reflexive desire to understand what it actually *feels like* to be the other person, in their body, with their history, their fears, their secret joys and private shames.

If that single switch flipped in enough minds, almost everything else we argue about would transform from the inside out. Politics would become less about domination and more about coordination. Education would stop being about rote obedience and start being about nurturing that curiosity. Technology would be shaped by people who actually wonder what it’s like to live with the consequences of what they build. Even personal relationships would change, because you can’t stay cruel or indifferent for long once you’re addicted to understanding.

Everything cascades from that one trait. Cruelty requires a lack of curiosity. Injustice survives on it. Most of humanity’s worst habits are, at root, failures of imagination.

So yeah. One change.

Make us insatiably, uncomfortably, beautifully curious about each other. The rest would mostly sort itself out.
